channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan Security Forces Conduct Successful Operations Against India-Sponsored Terrorists in KP
item.description: Pakistan’s security forces carried out multiple high-intensity, intelligence-based operations across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on 6th and 7th March, successfully neutralising 13 terrorists reportedly backed ...

item.title: Exploring Malta in the Off-Season: History, Architecture, and Local Life
item.description: Traveling to Malta in January offers a unique experience, free from the usual tourist crowds and high prices of the peak season. The author describes arriving at night during rainy conditions and rent...

item.title: Trump States No Interest in Negotiating with Iran, Suggests War May End Only After Destruction of Its Military and Leadership
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump, speaking aboard Air Force One, declared he has no interest in negotiating with Iran amid the ongoing U.S.-Israeli air campaign now in its second week. He suggested the con...

item.title: Chief of Defence Staff discusses AI’s role in future military decision-making
item.description: At the Raisina Dialogue 2026, Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan emphasised that artificial intelligence (AI) is set to play a transformative role in the future of warfare. He highlighted tha...

item.title: Court Case Over DNA Test for Child Born Through IVF Embryo Mix-Up
item.description: A black couple from Ballito has approached the courts seeking a DNA test on an eight-year-old boy, claiming the child was born from their embryo, which they allege was mistakenly given to an Indian wo...

item.title: Australians Evacuated from Middle East Amid Conflict, Some Paying Hefty Sums for Border Crossings
item.description: Amid the escalating US-Israeli-Iranian conflict, over 1,300 Australians have been evacuated from the Middle East since Wednesday via eight flights, though some flights have returned with empty seats. ...

item.title: Police Strengthen Counter-Terrorism Units with Training and New Bases in Three States
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force (NPF) don train 56 officers and approve new counter-terrorism bases for Adamawa, Cross River and Ogun states. Dis one na part of effort to make sure say dem fit respond well t...

item.title: Prominent Kenyan Leaders Who Lost Their Lives in Air Accidents
item.description: Over the years, Kenya has mourned the loss of several influential political and military leaders due to tragic aircraft accidents. The most recent incident claimed the life of Emurua Dikirr MP Johanna...

item.title: Dunblane Massacre: How the 1996 School Shooting Led to Britain's Handgun Ban
item.description: On 13 March 1996, Thomas Hamilton entered Dunblane Primary School in Scotland and murdered 16 children aged five and six, along with their teacher Gwen Mayor, in the gymnasium during a PE lesson. He i...

item.title: Western Cape Judge Ordered to Vacate State-Owned Home
item.description: Western Cape High Court judge Daniel Thulare has been ordered to vacate a state-owned property in Cape Town by April 15. Thulare had been occupying the home unlawfully for several years, paying signif...

item.title: University of Karachi shifts morning classes online till end of Ramazan
item.description: The University of Karachi (KU) has announced that all morning classes will be conducted online from Monday, March 9, 2026, until the conclusion of Ramazan. This decision comes as a relief for students...

item.title: Man Arrested for Shooting at Parañaque Condo Basketball Court, 2 Dead, 4 Injured
item.description: Authorities in Metro Manila arrested a 59-year-old man, William de Guzman, in connection with a deadly shooting that occurred at a condominium basketball court in Parañaque City on March 4, 2026. The ...

item.title: Sydney man dies from stabbing during suspected domestic dispute, another arrested
item.description: A man in his 30s has tragically died after being stabbed in a home in Sydney’s inner west, in what police believe was a domestic-related incident. Authorities were called to a residence on Spring Stre...

item.title: Prince William and Kate tour Marlborough College with George and Charlotte amid speculation over secondary school choice
item.description: Speculation about Prince George's secondary education has intensified following reports of a family visit to Marlborough College in Wiltshire. Prince William, the Princess of Wales, Prince George (12)...

item.title: Nadia Nakai Clarifies Relationship Rumours and Discusses Tropika Island of Treasure
item.description: Rapper Nadia Nakai has set the record straight regarding her social media buzz with Toss, confirming that they are not romantically involved. The speculation followed a Valentine’s Day video in which ...

item.title: Toronto Police Officer Sustains Injuries in West End Traffic Collision
item.description: A Toronto police officer was injured in a traffic collision with another driver in the city’s west end. Emergency services responded promptly to the scene, and the officer was transported to a local h...

item.title: Niger Delta Games Highlight Sports’ Role in Youth Empowerment and Community Development
item.description: Director General of the National Sports Commission, Bukola Olopade, talk say sports no be only about who go collect medal, but e dey involve passion sharing, community engagement, economic empowerment...

item.title: King Charles highlights Commonwealth's unifying role amid global conflicts and climate challenges in annual message
item.description: In his Commonwealth Day message, King Charles III emphasises the importance of traditional alliances and international friendships during a period of 'increasing pressures of conflict', referencing on...

item.title: Minor explosion damages US embassy entrance in Oslo, no casualties: Norwegian police investigate
item.description: An explosion occurred at the US embassy in Oslo, Norway, in the early hours of Sunday, March 8, 2026, around 1am local time. According to Oslo police, the blast caused only minor material damage to on...

item.title: Sialkot Petrol Station Worker Killed, Two Injured After Refusing to Fill Jerry Cans
item.description: In Sialkot, a petrol station incident turned fatal after the government announced a Rs55 per litre hike in petrol prices. Hassan Khan, a worker at a filling station in Hajipura, refused to fill jerry ...
